audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Miriam, Aaron, Moses, and Cushite-wife narrative tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Cushite-wife criticism, prophetic authority, Moses' humility, Divine speech, Miriam's skin affliction, Aaron's confession, Moses' healing prayer, seven-day exclusion, and communal waiting. Strong anti-racism, anti-misogyny, anti-authoritarian, and anti-stigma safeguards required.

verses.restored: Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses concerning the Cushite woman whom he had taken, for a Cushite woman he had taken.
They said, Has YHWH indeed spoken only through Moses? Has he not also spoken through us? And YHWH heard.
Now the man Moses was very humble/meek, more than every human upon face of ground.
Suddenly YHWH said to Moses and to Aaron and to Miriam, You three go out to tent of meeting. And the three went out.
YHWH came down in pillar of cloud and stood at entrance of tent, and called Aaron and Miriam; the two went forward.
He said, Hear, please, my words: If there is a prophet among you, I YHWH make myself known to him in vision; in dream I speak with him.
Not so my servant Moses; in all my house he is faithful.
Mouth to mouth I speak with him, plainly and not in riddles, and form of YHWH he beholds. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant, against Moses?
Anger of YHWH burned against them, and he departed.
Cloud turned away from over tent, and look, Miriam was afflicted with scale-disease, white as snow. Aaron turned toward Miriam, and look, she was afflicted.
Aaron said to Moses, Please, my lord, do not place upon us sin/misalignment in which we acted foolishly and in which we sinned.
Please do not let her be like one dead, whose flesh is half consumed when coming out from mother's womb.
Moses cried to YHWH, saying, El, please heal her, please.
YHWH said to Moses, If her father had spit in her face, would she not bear shame seven days? Let her be shut outside camp seven days, and afterward she shall be gathered in.
Miriam was shut outside camp seven days, and people did not set out until Miriam was gathered in.
Afterward people set out from Hazeroth and camped in wilderness of Paran.

translation_notes: The Cushite woman must not be demeaned or treated as the cause of impurity. The text gives no license for racism, anti-Blackness, xenophobia, or condemnation of interracial marriage.
Miriam and Aaron both speak, but only Miriam receives the visible affliction. This gender asymmetry must remain under ethical and reception-history critique.
Moses' unique prophetic role in this narrative does not create blanket immunity from accountability; Torah elsewhere records challenges, failures, and correction of leaders.
The affliction is rendered scale-disease/skin affliction rather than modern Hansen's disease.
Moses' brief prayer, El na refa na lah, centers healing rather than retaliation.
The whole community waits seven days for Miriam. Temporary exclusion does not become abandonment.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 12 to silence women, punish criticism of leaders, defend racism, reject interracial families, stigmatize skin disease, or claim that illness proves Divine displeasure. Preserve the narrative while centering healing, communal waiting, and critical attention to its gender asymmetry.
